title: Introduction
content:
## Purpose and Scope

This Digital Adaptation Kit (DAK) provides implementation guidance for the Core Information Standard v2.0, a comprehensive data model designed to support person-centred care across health and social care settings in the UK. The standard facilitates the consistent capture, sharing, and use of essential personal information to improve care coordination and outcomes.

The Core Information Standard encompasses a wide range of data elements including person demographics, clinical information, care planning, social context, and administrative details. It supports the recording of both structured and unstructured data, enabling healthcare professionals to maintain comprehensive records while ensuring interoperability between systems.

## Background

The standard was developed using WRIST (Wonderful Ramsey Systems Information Specification Tool) to ensure robust data modelling and validation. It aligns with NHS Data Dictionary standards, FHIR R4 profiles, and established clinical terminologies including SNOMED CT, LOINC, and dm+d.

This DAK serves as a practical guide for organisations implementing the standard, providing technical specifications, clinical guidance, and practical examples to support successful deployment across diverse healthcare environments.

title: Health Interventions & Recommendations
content:
## Key Clinical Recommendations

The Core Information Standard supports evidence-based care through structured data capture that enables clinical decision support and quality improvement initiatives.

| Intervention | Recommendation | Source | Grade |
|---|---|---|---|
| Person-centred care planning | Record individual goals, preferences and outcomes using structured templates | NICE NG86 | Strong |
| Information sharing | Implement consent frameworks with granular controls for data sharing | Caldicott Principles | Strong |
| Risk assessment | Document risks using standardised scales and SNOMED CT terminology | NICE Guidelines | Strong |
| Medication reconciliation | Maintain accurate medication records using dm+d codes | NICE NG5 | Strong |
| Safeguarding procedures | Record safeguarding concerns and actions using structured fields | NICE PH50 | Strong |
| Advance care planning | Document preferences for future care including DNACPR decisions | GMC Guidelines | Strong |

## Implementation Context

These recommendations are embedded within the standard's data model through mandatory and optional fields, coded value sets, and structured templates. The standard supports quality indicators and outcome measurement through its comprehensive data capture capabilities.

title: Generic Personas
content:
## Implementation Personas

| Persona ID | Occupational Title | Description | Targeted | Key Competencies |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.P1 | Clinical Data Manager | Manages clinical data standards and governance | Yes | Data modelling, clinical terminology, FHIR standards |
| CISV2.P2 | Health Informatics Specialist | Designs and implements health information systems | Yes | System integration, interoperability, HL7 FHIR |
| CISV2.P3 | GP Practice Manager | Oversees primary care practice operations | Yes | Clinical workflows, patient data management, NHS requirements |
| CISV2.P4 | Social Care Coordinator | Coordinates social care services and planning | Yes | Care planning, assessment tools, multi-agency working |
| CISV2.P5 | Hospital Systems Analyst | Analyses and optimises hospital information systems | Yes | Healthcare IT, system analysis, process improvement |
| CISV2.P6 | Community Nurse | Provides nursing care in community settings | Yes | Clinical assessment, care coordination, documentation |

### Clinical Data Manager (CISV2.P1)
Responsible for ensuring data quality and compliance with clinical standards. They work closely with clinical teams to define data requirements and maintain terminology mappings. Key focus areas include SNOMED CT implementation, data validation rules, and ensuring regulatory compliance across all clinical records.

### Health Informatics Specialist (CISV2.P2)
Leads technical implementation of interoperability standards and system integrations. They design APIs, configure FHIR endpoints, and ensure seamless data exchange between systems. Their expertise spans both technical architecture and clinical workflow optimisation.

### GP Practice Manager (CISV2.P3)
Oversees implementation of the standard within primary care settings, ensuring it supports existing workflows and meets practice needs. They coordinate training programmes and manage change processes to ensure successful adoption by clinical staff.

### Social Care Coordinator (CISV2.P4)
Focuses on care planning and assessment components of the standard, ensuring social care needs are accurately captured and shared. They work with multi-disciplinary teams to implement person-centred planning approaches and outcome measurement frameworks.

### Hospital Systems Analyst (CISV2.P5)
Analyses hospital workflows and system requirements to optimise implementation of the standard. They identify integration points, design data flows, and ensure the standard supports clinical decision-making and quality improvement initiatives.

### Community Nurse (CISV2.P6)
Provides practical input on clinical data requirements and usability aspects of the standard. They ensure documentation supports clinical practice and contributes to improved patient outcomes through better care coordination and information sharing.

title: References
content:
## Standards and Specifications

1. [NHS Data Dictionary](https://datadictionary.nhs.uk/) - National data standards and definitions
2. [HL7 FHIR R4](https://www.hl7.org/fhir/r4/) - Healthcare interoperability standard
3. [UK Core FHIR Implementation Guide](https://simplifier.net/ukcore) - UK-specific FHIR profiles
4. [SNOMED CT UK Edition](https://termbrowser.nhs.uk/) - Clinical terminology system

## Clinical Guidelines

5. NICE NG86 (2018). [Care and support planning for people with long-term conditions](https://www.nice.org.uk/guidance/ng86)
6. NICE NG5 (2015). [Medicines optimisation: the safe and effective use of medicines](https://www.nice.org.uk/guidance/ng5)
7. NICE PH50 (2014). [Domestic violence and abuse: multi-agency working](https://www.nice.org.uk/guidance/ph50)
8. [Caldicott Principles](https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/the-caldicott-principles) - Information governance framework

## Professional Standards

9. [GMC Treatment and care towards the end of life](https://www.gmc-uk.org/ethical-guidance/ethical-guidance-for-doctors/treatment-and-care-towards-the-end-of-life)
10. [Professional Records Standards Body](https://theprsb.org/) - Clinical documentation standards

## Technical Resources

11. [Organisation Data Service (ODS)](https://digital.nhs.uk/services/organisation-data-service) - Healthcare organisation codes
12. [Dictionary of medicines and devices (dm+d)](https://www.nhsbsa.nhs.uk/pharmacies-gp-practices-and-appliance-contractors/dictionary-medicines-and-devices-dmd) - Medicines terminology
13. [Unified Code for Units of Measure (UCUM)](https://ucum.org/) - International units standard

title: Business Processes
content:
## Business Processes

The Core Information Standard v2.0 supports key business processes across health and social care settings. These processes enable person-centred care through structured data capture, sharing, and utilisation.

Each process involves multiple personas working collaboratively to capture, validate, and share essential information that supports care coordination and decision-making. The processes are designed to ensure compliance with clinical governance requirements while maintaining focus on individual needs and preferences.

processes_data.summary_markdown: The Core Information Standard supports five core business processes that enable person-centred care coordination across health and social care settings. These processes ensure comprehensive information capture, validation, sharing, and utilisation while maintaining clinical governance and individual consent frameworks.

activities.title: Person Registration and Demographics Capture
activities.personas: Clinical Data Manager, GP Practice Manager, Health Informatics Specialist
activities.objectives: Establish complete person identity and demographic record with verified NHS number and contact details

nodes.label: Verify person identity and NHS number

activities.title: Consent and Information Sharing Management
activities.personas: Clinical Data Manager, Social Care Coordinator, Health Informatics Specialist
activities.objectives: Establish and maintain valid consent frameworks for information sharing across care settings

activities.title: Clinical Data Capture and Validation
activities.personas: Community Nurse, Clinical Data Manager, Hospital Systems Analyst
activities.objectives: Capture comprehensive clinical information including problems, medications, and care plans with appropriate validation

nodes.label: Verify allergy and adverse reaction status

activities.title: Multi-disciplinary Care Coordination
activities.personas: Social Care Coordinator, Community Nurse, GP Practice Manager, Health Informatics Specialist
activities.objectives: Coordinate care across multiple services with shared care planning and professional communication

activities.title: Secure Information Sharing Between Services
activities.personas: Health Informatics Specialist, Clinical Data Manager, GP Practice Manager, Hospital Systems Analyst
activities.objectives: Enable secure sharing of person-centred information across health and social care boundaries with appropriate access controls

title: User Scenarios
content:
## New Patient Registration and Initial Assessment

Sarah, a 45-year-old woman, moves to a new area and registers with a local GP practice. She has multiple chronic conditions including diabetes and depression, takes several medications, and has specific communication needs due to hearing impairment.

**Personas involved:** Clinical Data Manager (CISV2.P1), GP Practice Manager (CISV2.P3), Community Nurse (CISV2.P6)

**Linked processes:** CISV2.A, CISV2.C

**Data elements involved:**

| Element ID | Label | Used For |
|---|---|---|
| CISV2.DE4 | Person first name | Patient identification |
| CISV2.DE5 | Person family name | Patient identification |
| CISV2.DE10 | Date of birth | Demographics verification |
| CISV2.DE15 | NHS number | Unique patient identifier |
| CISV2.DE19 | Address line 1 | Current residential address |
| CISV2.DE24 | Postcode | Address validation and locality |
| CISV2.DE29 | Person's telephone number | Contact information |
| CISV2.DE58 | How I communicate and how to communicate with me | Recording hearing impairment needs |
| CISV2.DE78 | Reasonable adjustment | Documenting hearing aid requirements |
| CISV2.DE654 | Problem | Recording diabetes and depression diagnoses |
| CISV2.DE1330 | Medication name | Capturing current medications |

## Multi-disciplinary Care Planning for Complex Needs

Tom, a 28-year-old man with learning disabilities and autism, requires coordinated care planning across health and social care services. His support network includes family carers, a social worker, community nurse, and psychiatrist.

**Personas involved:** Social Care Coordinator (CISV2.P4), Community Nurse (CISV2.P6), Health Informatics Specialist (CISV2.P2)

**Linked processes:** CISV2.D, CISV2.B

**Data elements involved:**

| Element ID | Label | Used For |
|---|---|---|
| CISV2.DE54 | What is most important to me | Person-centred planning foundation |
| CISV2.DE56 | People who are important to me | Identifying key support network |
| CISV2.DE105 | Impairment category | Documenting learning disability |
| CISV2.DE131 | Cognition | Recording cognitive assessment |
| CISV2.DE215 | Consent for information sharing | Managing capacity and consent |
| CISV2.DE454 | Name | Professional team members |
| CISV2.DE455 | Role | Professional responsibilities |
| CISV2.DE1437 | Needs, concerns or problems | Care planning assessment |
| CISV2.DE1441 | Goals and hopes | Person-centred outcomes |
| CISV2.DE1445 | What | Planned care activities |
| CISV2.DE1448 | Who | Responsible professionals |

## Emergency Care Information Sharing

Maria, an 82-year-old woman with dementia, is brought to A&E following a fall. The emergency team needs immediate access to her medication list, allergies, and care preferences to provide safe treatment.

**Personas involved:** Hospital Systems Analyst (CISV2.P5), Health Informatics Specialist (CISV2.P2), Clinical Data Manager (CISV2.P1)

**Linked processes:** CISV2.E, CISV2.C

**Data elements involved:**

| Element ID | Label | Used For |
|---|---|---|
| CISV2.DE199 | Alert | Critical safety information display |
| CISV2.DE262 | Mental capacity assessment | Understanding decision-making capacity |
| CISV2.DE1268 | Causative agent | Identifying allergic reactions |
| CISV2.DE1279 | Description of reaction | Allergy severity information |
| CISV2.DE1330 | Medication name | Current medication reconciliation |
| CISV2.DE1359 | Dose directions description | Safe prescribing information |
| CISV2.DE1542 | Advance statement | Treatment preferences and wishes |
| CISV2.DE1622 | Cardio-pulmonary resuscitation (CPR) decision | End-of-life care decisions |
| CISV2.DE467 | Name | Emergency contact identification |
| CISV2.DE468 | Relationship | Contact relationship to patient |

## Care Coordination During Hospital Discharge

James, a 67-year-old man recovering from stroke, requires coordinated discharge planning involving occupational therapy, social care assessment, and ongoing rehabilitation services in the community.

**Personas involved:** Hospital Systems Analyst (CISV2.P5), Social Care Coordinator (CISV2.P4), GP Practice Manager (CISV2.P3)

**Linked processes:** CISV2.D, CISV2.E

**Data elements involved:**

| Element ID | Label | Used For |
|---|---|---|
| CISV2.DE558 | Date of discharge | Discharge planning timeline |
| CISV2.DE572 | Discharge destination | Care transition planning |
| CISV2.DE155 | Mobility needs | Ongoing care requirements |
| CISV2.DE1401 | Equipment name | Assistive equipment provision |
| CISV2.DE1419 | Actions for professionals | Multi-agency coordination |
| CISV2.DE1420 | Actions for person or their carer | Self-care guidance |
| CISV2.DE576 | Date of appointment | Follow-up care scheduling |
| CISV2.DE585 | Professional to see the person | Ongoing care team identification |
| CISV2.DE1003 | Social care package name | Community support services |
| CISV2.DE1005 | Service start date | Care package implementation |

## Mental Health Crisis Response

Alex, a 34-year-old person with bipolar disorder, experiences a mental health crisis. The crisis team needs rapid access to their mental health history, current medications, crisis plan, and emergency contacts.

**Personas involved:** Health Informatics Specialist (CISV2.P2), Clinical Data Manager (CISV2.P1), Community Nurse (CISV2.P6)

**Linked processes:** CISV2.E, CISV2.C

**Data elements involved:**

| Element ID | Label | Used For |
|---|---|---|
| CISV2.DE1242 | Risk | Current risk assessment status |
| CISV2.DE1508 | Trigger factors | Crisis identification factors |
| CISV2.DE1514 | What should happen | Crisis response actions |
| CISV2.DE1517 | Who should be contacted | Emergency contact protocols |
| CISV2.DE1531 | Coping strategy | Personal coping mechanisms |
| CISV2.DE1535 | Relapse indicators / early warning signs | Crisis prevention monitoring |
| CISV2.DE1330 | Medication name | Current psychiatric medications |
| CISV2.DE333 | Mental health act or equivalent status | Legal status assessment |
| CISV2.DE654 | Problem | Mental health diagnosis |
| CISV2.DE467 | Name | Crisis support contacts |

scenarios_metadata.title: New Patient Registration and Initial Assessment
Multi-disciplinary Care Planning for Complex Needs

title: Decision-Support Logic
content:
## Consent and Capacity Decision Support

### Mental Capacity Assessment Table

**Description:** Determines appropriate information sharing permissions based on mental capacity assessment and legal framework.

| Input: Capacity Assessment (CISV2.DE262) | Input: Age | Input: Legal Status (CISV2.DE333) | Output Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| Has capacity | ≥18 years | No MHA section | Obtain direct consent (CISV2.DE215) |
| Has capacity | ≥16 years | No MHA section | Obtain direct consent with parental involvement |
| Lacks capacity | Any age | No MHA section | Check for LPA/Deputy (CISV2.DE291) |
| Lacks capacity | <16 years | No MHA section | Obtain parental consent (CISV2.DE239) |
| Variable capacity | Any age | MHA sectioned | Apply MHA framework for information sharing |

### Risk Assessment and Safety Planning

**Description:** Triggers appropriate safety measures based on risk level and safeguarding indicators.

| Input: Safeguarding Flag (CISV2.DE380) | Input: Risk Level (CISV2.DE1243) | Input: Age | Output Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| True | High | <18 years | Initiate child protection procedures (CISV2.DE423) |
| True | High | ≥18 years | Adult safeguarding referral (CISV2.DE387) |
| False | High | Any age | Create safety plan (CISV2.DE1510) |
| False | Medium | Any age | Enhanced monitoring and review |
| False | Low | Any age | Standard care pathway |

## Clinical Decision Support

### Medication Safety Checks

**Description:** Validates medication prescribing against known allergies and contraindications.

| Input: Prescribed Drug (CISV2.DE1330) | Input: Known Allergy (CISV2.DE1268) | Input: Severity (CISV2.DE1283) | Output Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| Drug class match | Any allergen | Severe/Life-threatening | Block prescription, alert prescriber |
| Drug class match | Any allergen | Moderate | Warning dialogue, require override |
| Cross-reactivity risk | Related substance | Any severity | Caution alert with guidance |
| No known interaction | No relevant allergy | N/A | Proceed with standard checks |

### Care Planning Priority Matrix

**Description:** Prioritizes care actions based on person preferences and clinical need.

| Input: Goal Importance (CISV2.DE1442) | Input: Clinical Priority | Input: Person Preference | Output Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| High (8-10) | High | Strongly agrees | Immediate implementation (CISV2.DE1445) |
| High (8-10) | Medium | Agrees | Schedule within 2 weeks |
| Medium (5-7) | High | Neutral | Clinical discussion required |
| Low (1-4) | Low | Disagrees | Defer or alternative approach |

### Assessment Trigger Logic

**Description:** Determines when structured assessments should be initiated based on clinical indicators.

| Input: Problem Category (CISV2.DE654) | Input: Impairment Type (CISV2.DE105) | Input: Service Context | Output Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| Mental health | Cognitive | Community | Initiate capacity assessment (CISV2.DE262) |
| Mobility issues | Physical | Discharge planning | Mobility assessment (CISV2.DE155) |
| Communication needs | Sensory | Any setting | Communication assessment (CISV2.DE58) |
| Multiple conditions | Long-term condition | Care planning | Comprehensive assessment (CISV2.DE1186) |

title: Indicators & Performance Metrics
content:
## Quality and Safety Indicators

| Indicator ID | Title | Numerator | Denominator | Disaggregation | Frequency |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.IND.01 | Consent Documentation Rate | Patients with documented consent status (CISV2.DE215) | All active patients | By service type, age group | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND.02 | Mental Capacity Assessment Compliance | Patients lacking capacity with completed assessment (CISV2.DE262) | Patients identified as lacking capacity | By care setting, professional type | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND.03 | Reasonable Adjustment Implementation | Patients with documented adjustments (CISV2.DE78) implemented | Patients requiring reasonable adjustments (CISV2.DE76) | By adjustment type, care setting | Quarterly |
| CISV2.IND.04 | Allergy Alert Completeness | Patients with documented allergy reactions (CISV2.DE1279) | Patients with recorded allergies (CISV2.DE1268) | By severity level, substance type | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND.05 | Safeguarding Response Time | Safeguarding concerns with timely response | Total safeguarding concerns raised (CISV2.DE387) | By age group, concern type | Monthly |

## Care Coordination Indicators

| Indicator ID | Title | Numerator | Denominator | Disaggregation | Frequency |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.IND.06 | Care Plan Completeness | Patients with goals (CISV2.DE1441) and actions (CISV2.DE1445) documented | Patients requiring care plans | By service type, complexity level | Quarterly |
| CISV2.IND.07 | Professional Contact Currency | Contacts with review dates within 6 months (CISV2.DE464) | All professional contacts (CISV2.DE453) | By professional role, service type | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND.08 | Information Sharing Effectiveness | Referrals with complete information transfer | Total referrals made (CISV2.DE478) | By urgency level, specialty | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND.09 | Medication Reconciliation Rate | Admissions with complete medication review (CISV2.DE1330) | Total admissions (CISV2.DE529) | By admission type, patient age | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND.10 | Discharge Planning Timeliness | Discharges with documented plan 48hrs before discharge | Total planned discharges (CISV2.DE558) | By discharge destination, length of stay | Weekly |

## Clinical Outcomes Indicators

| Indicator ID | Title | Numerator | Denominator | Disaggregation | Frequency |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.IND.11 | Goal Achievement Rate | Goals marked as achieved (CISV2.DE1471) | Total active goals set (CISV2.DE1441) | By goal type, timeframe | Quarterly |
| CISV2.IND.12 | Risk Reduction Effectiveness | Risks with reduced severity over time (CISV2.DE1243) | All identified high/medium risks (CISV2.DE1242) | By risk type, intervention type | Quarterly |
| CISV2.IND.13 | Assessment Tool Utilization | Structured assessments completed (CISV2.DE1189) | Patients requiring formal assessment | By assessment type, care setting | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND.14 | End of Life Preference Alignment | Deaths in preferred location (CISV2.DE1742) | Patients with documented preference | By place type, condition group | Quarterly |
| CISV2.IND.15 | Emergency Contact Accessibility | Emergency contacts reached within 4 hours | Emergency presentations requiring contact (CISV2.DE467) | By contact type, time of day | Monthly |

## Person-Centered Care Indicators

| Indicator ID | Title | Numerator | Denominator | Disaggregation | Frequency |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.IND.16 | Person Preference Documentation | Patients with "About Me" completed (CISV2.DE67) | All patients in care planning process | By age group, care setting | Quarterly |
| CISV2.IND.17 | Individual Requirements Met | Requirements with evidence of provision | Total individual requirements (CISV2.DE179) | By requirement type, service area | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND.18 | Communication Needs Addressed | Patients with communication plan implementation | Patients with communication needs (CISV2.DE58) | By need type, adjustment provided | Quarterly |
| CISV2.IND.19 | Cultural Preference Accommodation | Services aligned with person preferences | Patients with documented preferences (CISV2.DE12, CISV2.DE13) | By ethnic group, religious belief | Annual |
| CISV2.IND.20 | Care Plan Agreement Rate | Plans agreed by person/representative (CISV2.DE1473) | Total care plans created | By capacity status, plan complexity | Quarterly |

title: Functional & Non-Functional Requirements
content:
## Functional Requirements

| Req ID | Title | Description | Linked Processes | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.FR.01 | Person Registration | System must capture complete demographic information including NHS number validation and duplicate checking | CISV2.A | High |
| CISV2.FR.02 | Consent Management | System must manage granular consent preferences with audit trail and withdrawal capabilities | CISV2.B | High |
| CISV2.FR.03 | Mental Capacity Recording | System must capture capacity assessments with decision-specific evaluations and review dates | CISV2.B, CISV2.C | High |
| CISV2.FR.04 | Clinical Data Validation | System must validate clinical codes against SNOMED CT and provide terminology browsing capabilities | CISV2.C | High |
| CISV2.FR.05 | Allergy and Adverse Reaction Alerts | System must provide real-time alerts during prescribing and care planning based on recorded allergies | CISV2.C | Critical |
| CISV2.FR.06 | Multi-disciplinary Care Planning | System must support collaborative care planning with role-based access and responsibility assignment | CISV2.D | High |
| CISV2.FR.07 | Professional Contact Management | System must maintain current professional team details with automatic review reminders | CISV2.D | Medium |
| CISV2.FR.08 | Referral Tracking | System must track referral status, outcomes, and response times with workflow notifications | CISV2.D | High |
| CISV2.FR.09 | Information Sharing Controls | System must enforce information sharing rules based on consent, capacity, and legal framework | CISV2.E | Critical |
| CISV2.FR.10 | Document Management | System must store and retrieve multimedia documents with version control and confidentiality marking | CISV2.E | Medium |
| CISV2.FR.11 | Risk Assessment Workflow | System must guide risk assessment processes with automated escalation based on severity levels | CISV2.C, CISV2.D | High |
| CISV2.FR.12 | Medication Management | System must support structured medication recording with dose calculations and interaction checking | CISV2.C | High |
| CISV2.FR.13 | Assessment Tool Integration | System must support standardized assessment tools with automated scoring and trend analysis | CISV2.C | Medium |
| CISV2.FR.14 | Goal Setting and Tracking | System must enable SMART goal setting with progress monitoring and outcome measurement | CISV2.D | Medium |
| CISV2.FR.15 | Emergency Information Access | System must provide rapid access to critical information during emergency care episodes | CISV2.E | Critical |

## Non-Functional Requirements

| Req ID | Title | Description | Classification | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.NFR.01 | System Availability | System must maintain 99.5% uptime during core hours (08:00-18:00 Mon-Fri) and 99% outside core hours | Availability | High |
| CISV2.NFR.02 | Response Time Performance | System must respond to user queries within 3 seconds for data retrieval and 5 seconds for complex searches | Performance | High |
| CISV2.NFR.03 | Data Security Encryption | All data must be encrypted at rest using AES-256 and in transit using TLS 1.3 or higher | Security | Critical |
| CISV2.NFR.04 | Authentication and Authorization | System must implement role-based access control with multi-factor authentication for privileged users | Security | Critical |
| CISV2.NFR.05 | Audit Trail Completeness | System must maintain complete audit logs of all data access, modification, and sharing activities | Security | Critical |
| CISV2.NFR.06 | FHIR R4 Compliance | System must support FHIR R4 UK Core profiles for all clinical data exchanges | Interoperability | High |
| CISV2.NFR.07 | Terminology Standards | System must support SNOMED CT UK Edition, LOINC, dm+d, and ICD-10 terminology standards | Interoperability | High |
| CISV2.NFR.08 | Mobile Device Compatibility | System must function on tablets and smartphones with responsive design principles | Usability | Medium |
| CISV2.NFR.09 | Accessibility Compliance | System must meet WCAG 2.1 AA standards for accessibility including screen reader compatibility | Usability | High |
| CISV2.NFR.10 | Data Backup and Recovery | System must perform daily backups with 4-hour RTO and 30-minute RPO requirements | Reliability | High |
| CISV2.NFR.11 | Scalability | System must support concurrent usage by 10,000 users with linear performance scaling | Scalability | Medium |
| CISV2.NFR.12 | Integration Capability | System must provide RESTful APIs with rate limiting and monitoring for third-party integrations | Interoperability | High |
| CISV2.NFR.13 | Data Retention Policy | System must enforce configurable data retention periods with automated archival processes | Compliance | Medium |
| CISV2.NFR.14 | Disaster Recovery | System must support geographic redundancy with failover capabilities within 15 minutes | Reliability | High |
| CISV2.NFR.15 | Privacy by Design | System must implement privacy controls including data minimization, purpose limitation, and consent management | Privacy | Critical |
